structured_payload_candidate.title: Exhaust Fan Start/Stop - Option b: Cycle to Maintain Room Temperature
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Exhaust fan runs when zone temperature rises above active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Exhaust fan shall run until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.

evidence_quote: Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.
evidence_text: Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Room Temperature Control Method Application
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The room temperature control method should only be used in non-occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The room temperature control method should only be used in non-occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms).

evidence_quote: The room temperature control method should only be used in non-occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms).
evidence_text: The room temperature control method should only be used in non-occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Maintenance Interval Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm when the fan has operated for more than 3,000 hours. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fan has operated for more than 3,000 hours.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 3,000 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 3,000 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
evidence_text: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 3,000 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Alarm - Status/Command Mismatch
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. Commanded on, status off: Level 2. Commanded off, status off: Level 4.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Status differs from command for 15 seconds.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate alarm: Level 2 if commanded on and status off; Level 4 if commanded off and status off.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Status not matching command for 15 seconds.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2 b. Commanded off, status off: Level 4

evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2 b. Commanded off, status off: Level 4
